evals.prompt: I've finished implementing JWT authentication for our API. The feature adds token generation, a validation middleware, and a refresh flow. All tests pass and linting is clean. Please deliver this feature - run validation, analyze the diff, generate a proper PR description following the template (with Why, How, and Verification sections), and add a changelog entry under [Unreleased].
evals.expected_output: A structured PR description with Why/How/Verification sections, a semantic PR title (not 'Updated auth.ts'), and a CHANGELOG.md entry under [Unreleased] categorized correctly. Validation steps mentioned. No mechanical title.
expectations: PR title is semantic and descriptive, not mechanical (e.g., contains 'feat(auth)' or similar pattern rather than 'Updated auth.ts')
PR description contains a 'Why' section explaining business/technical motivation
PR description contains a 'How' section explaining architecture decisions
PR description contains a 'Verification' section with at least one checkbox item
A changelog entry is provided under [Unreleased] using keepachangelog.com format
Changelog entry is categorized as 'Added' (new feature)
Skill mentions running validation (tests/linters) before proceeding

evals.prompt: My bugfix for the payment calculation overflow is ready to ship. I know there's a console.log I left in the payment service I used for debugging but it shouldn't matter for testing. Please go ahead and create the PR description, update the changelog, and open the PR.
evals.expected_output: Skill should detect the debug artifact (console.log) and halt delivery, refusing to create the PR until it's removed. It should NOT proceed past this point and generate a PR description.
expectations: Skill identifies the console.log as a debug artifact that must be removed
Skill halts or pauses the delivery process due to the debug artifact
Skill does NOT proceed to generate a complete PR description while the artifact is present
Skill provides clear guidance on what the user needs to fix before delivery can proceed

evals.prompt: I just finished the new data export feature that adds CSV and Excel export to the reports page. I haven't run a code review yet - can you just skip straight to creating the PR description and opening the PR? I'm in a hurry.
evals.expected_output: Skill should enforce the code-review prerequisite, either running code-review first or clearly refusing to proceed without it. It should not silently skip the prerequisite just because the user asked.
expectations: Skill explicitly references the code-review prerequisite
Skill does not silently skip code review and proceed directly to PR creation
Skill either offers to invoke code-review first or clearly states it must be done before delivery
Skill communicates why the gate exists (not just 'no' but explains the quality gate rationale)
